meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Debug Code
content.seo_description: Learn how to master a professional client workflow to overcome debugging roadblocks. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at broken consoles. By adopting a systematic client-side debugging workflow, you transform errors into actionable data. Use Antigravity to architect your logic and Claude Code to iterate through stack traces. This dual-tool approach isolates bugs, validates state, and builds the professional resilience required for high-level software engineering.
content.intro:
The screen is a blur of red text. You’ve been staring at the same 'undefined is not a function' error for three hours, and the weight of impostor syndrome is starting to settle in. Every student faces this wall; the difference between a novice and a senior engineer isn't the absence of bugs, but the presence of a repeatable, clinical workflow.

Today, we stop guessing. We are going to treat your codebase like a professional production environment. By leveraging the analytical power of Antigravity and the agentic precision of Claude Code, you will transition from 'blindly fixing' to 'architecturally resolving' complex failures. Let’s turn that frustration into your greatest portfolio asset.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Domain
steps.description: Before touching code, define the boundaries. We use Antigravity to map the state transition that leads to the error, ensuring we aren't chasing ghosts in unrelated modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution flow. Identify the exact line where state diverges from expected schema and suggest a minimal reproduction test case.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the function responsible for the state mutation at line 42'

steps.title: Automate Trace Analysis
steps.description: Manual stack trace reading is a waste of cognitive load. Feed the output into Claude Code to identify the root cause based on historical patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the provided stack trace. Extract the dependency chain and pinpoint the breaking change in the data contract.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze the last three stack traces and summarize the recurring pattern in the network request layer'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Assertions
steps.description: Inject runtime checks to catch errors at the source. This prevents silent failures and makes debugging instantaneous in future iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of TypeScript assertions for the current data model that will throw immediate errors if the schema is violated.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add validation logic to the API response handler to catch null values before they reach the UI'

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases
steps.description: Most bugs hide in edge cases. We use our workflow to stress-test the system with malformed data inputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of edge-case inputs for this module, specifically focusing on empty state and unexpected API response formats.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a test script that mocks a 500 error and ensures the UI handles it gracefully'

steps.title: Refactor for Observability
steps.description: Finalize by adding structured logging. This turns your app into a self-documenting system that alerts you when things go wrong.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the error handling block to include structured logging that captures the full state object at the time of failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'replace all console.log statements with a structured logging utility that captures the user context'

pro_tips: Never fix a bug without writing a regression test; if the bug isn't in your test suite, it never really left your codebase.
Use Claude Code to explain the 'why' behind a fix, not just the 'what', to accelerate your personal learning curve.
Treat your error logs as a primary data source for product improvements, not just as a nuisance.
When stuck, force yourself to explain the bug to a rubber duck or an AI; the act of verbalizing the logic often reveals the flaw.
Maintain a 'Bug Journal' where you document the top 3 most difficult errors you solved this month to build your technical intuition.
content.next_steps: Take the current project you are building and apply these 5 steps to your most persistent bug. Once resolved, document the process in your portfolio README to demonstrate your professional debugging methodology to future employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact professional workflow used by senior engineers to ship stable, high-quality code. Stop fighting your editor and start mastering your environment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
